expectations.text: Identifies the populate restriction as partial-PK and explains it runs over every combination of the missing PK fields
expectations.evidence: Says 'restriction only pins one of the primary-key fields, so DataJoint will compute every missing-key combination it can find for that param name.'

expectations.text: Names the missing PK fields explicitly (`RippleLFPSelection`, `pos_merge_id`)
expectations.evidence: Lists the PK as 'RippleLFPSelection ... ripple_param_name ... pos_merge_id'.

expectations.text: Recommends building a fully-scoped `populate_key` with the missing fields included
Verifies the full PK with `code_graph.py describe RippleTimesV1` (agent tool) or `RippleTimesV1.heading.primary_key` in the user's Python session, not memory
expectations.evidence: Says 'Don't rely on memory' and recommends `RippleTimesV1.heading.primary_key` and `RippleTimesV1.describe()`.
